title: AIM Chains Output Schema (aim-chains-v1.0)
description: Public chains artefact emitted by the AIM Applier (pipeline/engine/apply_base.py → chains-YYYY-MM-DD.json + chains-latest.json). One chain per interpreter structured_claim, linking claim_id → per-claim review_status → patch_id (MVP: always null) → evidence (evidence_id + source_url + source_module_path). Designed for public audit of the monitor pipeline; replay-deterministic given the three upstream content SHAs. Sprint 3 Step 3 (AD-2026-04-23j).

chained_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the chain builder ran.

cycle_id.description: Canonical per-cycle identifier ({abbr}-YYYY-MM-DD). Matches the companion manifest's cycle_id.

chain_builder_deterministic.description: MVP guarantee: chain construction is pure deterministic stitching. Byte-identical replay given the three input SHAs + today's date.

chains.description: Ordered chain[] — one per interpreter structured_claim, preserving structured_claims[] order. chain_id format: '{abbr}-chain-{week_ending}-{NNNN}' (4-digit zero-padded index, 1-based).

claim_id.description: From interpret.structured_claims[].claim_id. Falls back to 'unknown-NNNN' if the source claim lacks an ID (observable as an interpret_schema_mismatch condition).

extraction_method.description: Derived from interpret claim.notes string (design §3.3 rule 3): 'deterministic' if notes contains 'deterministic', 'llm_fallback' if contains 'llm', else 'unknown'.

review_status.description: Per-claim status derived from brief-level review.verdict with per-claim escalation (design §3.3 rule 5): if review.claim_flags[] contains entries for this claim_id, APPROVE is promoted to APPROVE_WITH_CLAIM_FLAGS.

review_flags.description: Subset of review.claim_flags[] matching this claim_id (preserved verbatim). Empty array when none.

patch_id.description: MVP: always null. AMEND-D stub — Appendix-A-form patches deferred until the Interpreter emits patches in Appendix A shape (Step 5 or later).

patch_status.description: MVP: always 'no_patch_emitted'. Signals explicit absence of a patch for this claim in this cycle (not missing data).

source_url.description: Resolved via interpret.weekly_developments[] → cross_monitor_candidates[] → lead_signal.source_url fallback (design §3.3 rule 7). Null for deterministic claims extracted from persistent-state modules (typical).

source_module_path.description: Canonical module path for claims sourced from persistent-state (e.g. 'persistent-state/modules/…'). Null for weekly-input-sourced claims.

in_compose_trace.description: True iff compose.claim_trace[] referenced this claim_id (either as a string entry or as an object with matching claim_id). False for claims not surfaced in the published brief.
